title: SDL3 default breaks Wayland mid-game on NVIDIA (regression, looks like #6746)
author: Shruichan
body:
I installed the new update and a regression of some sort has made 
it impossible for me to play using the new update once the Flatpak pulled 2026.518.0-lazer, which
as far as i can tell is the first lazer build that ships #6746 (SDL3 default on linux).
Downgrading to 2026.429.0-lazer makes it go away. Hasn't happened once on the
older one.

What happens:
 I'll be playing, window just disappears. Audio keeps going and 
 I can hear the sounds from a multiplayer lobby I am in, the cursor too
but the audio is muffled, and i can only see my desktop. No crash dialog or anything
visible. And the process keeps running too, so I have to `pkill -9`
both `osu!` and the `bwrap` wrappers before I can launch again. Otherwise 
the realm lock blocks it.
All three times for me it was on a multiplayer screen. Twice in
multispectator, once on the multiplayer mod select. I didnt test
it in solo or song select, but lobby listing never crashed me.
So might be something about either starting a game, or actually playing
(more overlays going at once?, the game framerate spiking?) being the thing
that tips SDL3 over, but I'm guessing.

The line in the runtime log:

  ```
  2026-05-23 02:31:17 [verbose]: SDL video log [error]: Wayland display connection closed by server (fatal)
  ```

After that, the game keeps logging stuff. Beatmap polling, gameplay
clock updates, all the normal stuff. So video dies but the rest of the host
loop just keeps going, which is why the audio sticks around. Even if SDL3
wayland gets fixed, that "fatal" not actually bringing down the host feels
worth fixing on its own. A zombie process with audio leaking out of nowhere
is way worse UX than a clean crash.

To confirm it's actually the SDL3 switch:

  ```
  SDL3 Initialized
  SDL3 Version: 3.5.0
  SDL3 Video driver: wayland
  ```

The 429 build (SDL2 still) doesn't have any of this and runs fine forever.

Also tried switching the renderer to Vulkan on the broken build, just to
see. That stops the wayland disconnect but then it freezes hard on
MultiSpectatorResultsScreen after a round. And Vulkan using Borderless dies on
startup with:

  ```
  Veldrid.VeldridException: Could not acquire next image from the Vulkan swapchain.
  ```

So it's not GL-specific. SDL3 just doesn't seem happy on this stack at all.

 Setup:
  - osu!lazer 2026.518.0, Flatpak from Flathub
  - SDL3 3.5.0, wayland driver
  - Pop!_OS, COSMIC compositor, wayland session
  - RTX 5090, nvidia 580.126.18, also an AMD iGPU
  - 5120x1440 @ 240Hz, fullscreen, framesync unlimited

Workarounds:
  - I'm just on 2026.429.0 now. `flatpak update --commit=7609cb30... sh.ppy.osu`
    then `flatpak mask sh.ppy.osu` so it doesn't auto-upgrade back. Stable.
  - `SDL_VIDEODRIVER=x11` is no help under Flatpak fwiw. Manifest lists x11 in
    `sockets=` but SDL aborts with "x11 not available" anyway and the game
    won't even launch. Maybe XWayland isn't being auto-started by COSMIC,
    didn't dig further.

title: Android's `mobileffmpeg.so` and `mobileffmpeg-abidetect.so` are not needed.
author: shiumano
body:
https://github.com/ppy/osu-framework/pull/2865
https://github.com/ppy/osu-framework/commit/7bfd8e56d6605a4679e449524967935c8a61d41d

It was added in a commit 6 years ago.
The reason given was that ffmpeg was missing libraries necessary for it to function, but it seems only `libc++_shared.so` and `libcpufeatures.so` were needed, and mobileffmpeg was not required.

I investigated a bit because I was worried that deleting it might cause it to stop working.

- Searching for `mobileffmpeg` in the osu! source code yielded no results.
- MobileFFmpeg is for executing command-line commands like `-i input.mp4 output.mkv` from Android Java code. It's not needed in the osu! framework, which uses FFmpeg.AutoGen in C#. [(documentation)](https://tanersener.github.io/mobile-ffmpeg/#:~:text=Execute%20synchronous%20FFmpeg%20commands%2E)
- It's basically designed to be called via JNI (the function is defined with `JNIEXPORT jstring JNICALL`). It cannot be used in C#. [(source code)](https://github.com/tanersener/mobile-ffmpeg/blob/7a9c62f5563bff68da43d07e3286b5d8db50d53f/android/app/src/main/cpp/mobileffmpeg.h)
- It requires Java code for MobileFFmpeg to function, but it doesn't exist. An error is output to logcat. [(loader's source)](https://github.com/tanersener/mobile-ffmpeg/blob/7a9c62f5563bff68da43d07e3286b5d8db50d53f/android/app/src/main/cpp/mobileffmpeg_abidetect.c#L25-L53)
- There are no dependencies from other native libraries (`libav*` or other libraries). I checked with ldd, libtree, and strings. The only thing that's automatically loaded is `libxamarin-app.so` simply because "it's there."
 Incidentally, I confirmed that `libc++_shared.so` and `libcpufeatures.so` are requested.
- I actually tried deleting it, and everything worked fine, including GPU decoding via MediaCodec.

Furthermore, under certain conditions, it fails to start and crashes due to the JNI loading of mobileffmpeg-abidetect.

title: Osu sometimes won't open after SDL3 was enabled
author: cosstab
body:
After updating to Osu Tachyon 2026.512.0 on Arch Linux, I first noticed that I couldn't click with my tablet (as mentioned in #6749), so I restarted my PC and, after that, the game wouldn't even open multiple times in a row. This seems to happen sporadically now, the game opens most of the time.

title: Always use a global mixer
author: LastExceed
body:
Tracking issue for basically this comment:
https://github.com/ppy/osu-framework/blob/44c42076fa9f24db303283632483743261c198f8/osu.Framework/Audio/AudioManager.cs#L135-L146

We're already doing this with BASSWASAPI, so we should have everything we need in place to do the same for regular BASS playback without much effort.

It is unclear how exactly BASS handles parallel playback of independent channels (i.e. what we're currently doing). If they are kept separate (= multiple streams to the OS mixer), then we might have to watch out for some minor changes in offsets. But observing its winapi calls (using [api monitor](https://www.rohitab.com/apimonitor)) shows no increase in accesses to wasapi buffers when starting additional channels, so I *think* that BASS is essentially already using something akin to a global mixer anyway.

Currently blocks #705 , #6650, and ppy/osu#36296

title: Sample latency variance
author: LastExceed
body:
When a channel is added to a mixer stream, its first byte gets processed the next time data is retrieved from the mixer. This effectively causes the start of a channel to snap to the start of the next period, which introduces 0-1 periods of variance to the latency. E.g. when the audio engine uses a 10ms periodicity, then it takes anywhere between 0-10ms (depending on cycle alignment) until the channel's data begins to flow towards the output.

For tracks this isn't a problem, because the audio clock is based on the number of bytes that have been processed, so any initial delay affecting the track will by extension also affect the clock, and thus everything that's timed relative to that clock. shifting everything is the same as shifting nothing, so this is fine.

For samples however this means ±5ms uncertainty relative to an ongoing track, which is a problem. For instance, fully keysounded beatmaps tend to rely on seamless transitions between adjacent samples, so that any discontinuity between them reflects the temporal imprecision in the player's inputs. But right now, even auto-play has audible gaps/overlaps, unless the first sample's duration happens to be a perfect integer multiple of the audio update interval.

I still need to check if BASS has a mechanism for scheduling samples with an offset for intra-period precision, but if not, then a possible workaround would be to prepend samples with 1 period worth of silence, and then skip `1 period - time since last update` of that silence when registering the sample for playback. Alternatively we could try to configure a stupidly fast update rate in order to reduce the variance to irrelevance

title: Add environment variables to control BASS variables
author: Szwagi
body:
I am trying to make the lowest audio latency osu! setup possible under Linux.

I got down to 8ms of audio latency, then I thought to test a build of the game with `Bass.UpdatePeriod`, `Bass.DeviceBufferLength` and `Bass.PlaybackBufferLength` all set to 1 (I honestly do not know if this is even applied because these numbers don't make much sense).

This dropped my audio latency by 1.5ms to 6.5ms.

This is still pretty far away from 5ms I got using an ALSA app I made myself and 4ms that I got when measuring ASIO on Windows (audio interface + skill issue, mostly skill issue).
However, 1.5ms is still a big win for anyone trying to do this, and it'd be great if we can adjust these values.

EDIT: I have originally closed this when I found that using Pipewire gets me to ~5.5ms.
However, after thinking about this for a bit, this makes no sense to me.
It's worth noting these variables only affect latency on ALSA devices...
Why would `Game -> BASS -> Pipewire -> ALSA` have lower latency than `Game -> BASS -> ALSA`?
ALSA through BASS seems to add buffering that isn't needed when going through Pipewire.

title: Enable other WMV decoders in ffmpeg
author: longnguyen2004
body:
There's a [ranked map](https://osu.ppy.sh/beatmapsets/2611#osu/21761) that uses a WMV3 video, and WMV3 isn't enabled in the custom ffmpeg build, so currently the video doesn't play in osu!lazer

```
2025-08-19 13:47:41 [verbose]: VideoDecoder faulted: System.InvalidOperationException: No usable decoder found for codec ID AV_CODEC_ID_WMV3
2025-08-19 13:47:41 [verbose]: at osu.Framework.Graphics.Video.VideoDecoder.recreateCodecContext()
2025-08-19 13:47:41 [verbose]: at osu.Framework.Graphics.Video.VideoDecoder.StartDecoding()
```

`wmv3` would need to be enabled, and maybe `wmv1` as well for good measure, in case other videos exist that use that codec.

title: `GameThread.Clock.CurrentTime` is set at the end of a frame
author: Susko3
body:
- Noticed when looking into https://github.com/ppy/osu-framework/issues/4808

I would expect the current time to be set when a new frame begins.

This doesn't matter in multi-threaded mode, as a new frame begins immediately after the previous ends.

But I think it may cause unnecessary animation jitter in single thread mode. Probably less visible if all threads have constant frame time. It's really hard to think about this "CurrentTime" which is not current, but in the past; but it's always in the past, so it kinda works out.

Code that is updating the clock at the end:

https://github.com/ppy/osu-framework/blob/3dbcf0154797ee8977b6278c91c8071421210b21/osu.Framework/Threading/GameThread.cs#L457-L461

title: `TextBuilder` does not preserve weight when falling back to other fonts
author: frenzibyte
body:
Initially reported long ago in https://github.com/ppy/osu/discussions/14679#discussioncomment-1297572 but haven't got around opening a framework-side issue thread yet it seems.

Simply the `TextBuilder.getTexturedGlyph` method cannot fall back to other fonts without losing weight specification, as the weight specification is part of the font name. Major restructuring of the glyph lookup system may be required, at least splitting weight away from `FontName` and/or allowing it to be a point of lookup.

title: The `Game.Window.Resizable` setter on Android causes unexpected screen rotation
author: Frederisk

title: Orientation settings in MAUI Android are not working
author: Frederisk
body:
In the Android project, the game's `Android.App.ActivityAttribute.ScreenOrientation` property, and the `RequestedOrientation` property in the `AndroidGameActivity.OnCreate` method, have no effect on screen orientation.

Essentially, after debugging, I found that they actually all execute as expected. However, SDL exhibits an unexpected behavior (at least for me, as I couldn't find any mention in o!f documentation, nor any usage examples or comments in osu!) where if `SDL_HINT_ORIENTATIONS` is not assigned any valid value, `SDLActivity` will assign a [default value](https://github.com/libsdl-org/SDL/blob/d16371b923fed57e5df676a84435baf1d1061a6a/android-project/app/src/main/java/org/libsdl/app/SDLActivity.java#L1125) for orientation, which is typically `FullUser`. This assignment occurs after `OnCreate`, thus overriding any previous assignments.

Specifically, this process is [initiated](https://github.com/ppy/osu-framework/blob/a7815796130dbf24194d76437599464999976f02/osu.Framework/Platform/SDL3/SDL3Window.cs#L218) by the `SDL3Window.Create()` method.

A viable solution is to add such a call in the `OnCreate` method:

```cs
SDL.SDL3.SDL_SetHint(SDL.SDL3.SDL_HINT_ORIENTATIONS, "LandscapeLeft LandscapeRight"u8);
```

However, it's clear that we cannot assign arbitrary desired values, such as `Nosensor`, to the program via the `SDL_HINT_ORIENTATIONS`.
Furthermore, mixing SDL mode declarations in MAUI might lead to some unexpected side effects, which I will describe in a subsequent issue.
And frankly, this approach seems somewhat counter-intuitive and ugly.

title: Metal renderer may lose 1/3 of frame rate and bring long latency
author: sona-mini-studio
body:
I've played osu! with Macs with ProMotion and Apple silicon for 2 months. *For most of time* I use the Metal renderer, I saw 80 fps (**1-2-paced** on 120 Hz display) and perceived pretty long latency. I used Metal HUD and Instruments (in Xcode) and proved this. 

Sometimes after an update, such symptom disappeared for a short time and the Metal renderer worked its best to reduce latency (the picture could be glitchy, though). However, it appeared again at the time point *I don't know yet*.

- Clearing caches (~/Library/Application Support/osu/caches) and reboot the system doesn't work.
- Using any frame limiter doesn't work. (Not including "VSync")
- Doesn't work no matter Multi-threading is on or off.
- Forcing macOS Game Mode by modifying "Info.plist" in app package.

By the way, I need hardware cursor despite its downsides. It seems to be both low-latency and V-Synced. Let me enable it in the Settings panel, please. I major in the standard mode. The cursor is almost the only latency-sensitive visual element I perceive. I know it's very common to hide hardware cursors in game industries…

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/27a68fd3-944a-47e3-b573-ce96645fa38b)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d3c9edf2-b1b4-4897-9d7b-12ff6f551b24)

### Logs

[runtime.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19863046/runtime.log)

### Environments

- macOS 15.3 ... 15.4.1
- Apple M4 chip
